response.justification: The candidate has a broad polyglot background and genuine geospatial interest (GISCollective, Open Green Map GIS work), but the core job requirement is modern C++ or Rust for systems-level routing/navigation pipelines, and the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with DLang. Their C/C++ exposure is limited to an older role writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor-thesis project, with no evidence of modern C++ idioms, performance-critical routing logic, or navigation tile processing. There is zero Rust experience mentioned. The geospatial work is data-collection and mapping tooling, not routing tile optimization or low-latency pipeline architecture of the kind required here.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure across multiple projects (GISCollective, Open Green Map GIS app, FOSS4G talk), showing authentic interest in the problem space
Demonstrated ability to build and operate low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
Broad systems breadth including Linux, distributed infrastructure, embedded/hardware (robotic arms, LabWindows), and DevOps — shows adaptability across the stack
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust expertise; C/C++ usage appears dated and peripheral, and neither language appears in the skills summary
Geospatial experience is data-collection and visualization focused, with no evidence of routing graph construction, tile optimization, or navigation guidance systems
Current trajectory (TypeScript, AI agents, MCP frameworks) is moving away from, not toward, the systems-level C++ work this role demands
